questions.question: Which meaning best matches וילך in וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: came
saw/understood
and he went
went

questions.question: Which grammar clue in מביתו matters for reading וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: מ = in, בית = house, ו = his
מ = and, בית = action, ו = plural
מ = from, בית = house, ו = her
מ = from, בית = house, ו = his
questions.correct_answer: מ = from, בית = house, ו = his
questions.explanation: מביתו is read as 'from his house'.

questions.question: What does the phrase unit מביתו אל העיר mean in וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: from his house to the man
from the city to his house
from his house to the city
from his house in the city
questions.correct_answer: from his house to the city
questions.explanation: מביתו אל העיר functions as a combined phrase unit in the pasuk.

questions.question: What role does מביתו play in וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: starting point
subject
destination
object being given
questions.explanation: מביתו gives where the movement starts.

questions.question: Why does the context support 'and he went' for וילך in וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: saw/understood
went
and he went
came
questions.explanation: The surrounding words make 'and he went' the best reading of וילך.

questions.question: What must be understood before the destination makes sense in וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: מביתו must be read as the starting point before אל העיר can be read as the destination
אל העיר must be read as the starting point before מביתו can be read as the destination
מביתו gives possession, while אל העיר alone gives the full movement
האיש must be read as the destination before וילך gives the action
questions.correct_answer: מביתו must be read as the starting point before אל העיר can be read as the destination
questions.explanation: The flow depends on reading source and destination together.

questions.question: Which meaning becomes impossible if מביתו is replaced by בביתו in וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: movement toward the destination would no longer be possible
movement away from that place would no longer be possible
possession by the same person would no longer be possible
the source clue would turn into a location-inside clue
questions.correct_answer: movement away from that place would no longer be possible
questions.explanation: Changing one grammar clue changes how the phrase or sentence functions.

questions.question: Which explanation is MOST complete for וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר?
choices: וילך gives the action and האיש gives the subject, but the direction is only implied
וילך gives the action, האיש gives the subject, מביתו gives the source, and אל העיר gives the destination
מביתו gives the destination and אל העיר gives the starting point
וילך means gave, and העיר is the object being given
questions.correct_answer: וילך gives the action, האיש gives the subject, מביתו gives the source, and אל העיר gives the destination
questions.explanation: The strongest answer accounts for the action, roles, and grammar clues together.

questions.question: What action does ויתן describe in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: went
took
and he gave
gave

questions.question: Which grammar clue in לבנו matters for reading ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: ל = and, בן = action, ו = plural
ל = to, בן = son, ו = her
ל = in, בן = son, ו = his
ל = to, בן = son, ו = his
questions.correct_answer: ל = to, בן = son, ו = his
questions.explanation: לבנו is read as 'to his son'.

questions.question: What does the phrase unit לחם לבנו mean in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: bread with his son
bread from his son
bread to the father
bread to his son
questions.explanation: לחם לבנו functions as a combined phrase unit in the pasuk.

questions.question: What role does לבנו play in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: main action
object
subject
receiver
questions.explanation: לבנו marks who receives or is directed toward the action.

questions.question: Why does the context support 'and he gave' for ויתן in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: gave
and he gave
went
took
questions.explanation: The surrounding words make 'and he gave' the best reading of ויתן.

questions.question: Which clue creates the dependency between object and receiver in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: לבנו must be read as the object before לחם can mark the receiver
האב must be read as the receiver before לחם gives the object
ויתן shows transfer, but לבנו only shows possession and not receiver
לחם must be read as the object before לבנו can mark the receiver
questions.correct_answer: לחם must be read as the object before לבנו can mark the receiver
questions.explanation: The flow depends on distinguishing the object from the receiver.

questions.question: What new meaning is introduced if לבנו is replaced by מבנו in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: the receiver clue would become a source clue
movement toward that person would become stronger
possession by that person would become the main meaning
movement away from that person would be introduced
questions.correct_answer: movement away from that person would be introduced
questions.explanation: Changing one grammar clue changes how the phrase or sentence functions.

questions.question: Which clue is MOST important for understanding who receives the action in ויתן האב לחם לבנו?
choices: לבנו gives the object, and לחם gives the receiver
ויתן means went, and לבנו gives the destination city
ויתן gives the action, האב gives the giver, לחם gives the object, and לבנו gives the receiver
ויתן gives the action and האב gives the giver, but the receiver is not marked
questions.correct_answer: ויתן gives the action, האב gives the giver, לחם gives the object, and לבנו gives the receiver
questions.explanation: The strongest answer accounts for the action, roles, and grammar clues together.

flows.pasuk: וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר / ויתן האב לחם לבנו / וישב האיש בביתו

questions.word: וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר / ויתן האב לחם לבנו / וישב האיש בביתו
questions.selected_word: וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר / ויתן האב לחם לבנו / וישב האיש בביתו
questions.question: Which sequence best follows the multi-pasuk flow?
choices: both actions happen at the same time because both begin with ו
there is no action in either pasuk
first וילך happens, then ויתן happens
first ויתן happens, then וילך happens
questions.correct_answer: first וילך happens, then ויתן happens
questions.explanation: The order of the pesukim controls the sequence of actions.

questions.word: וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר / ויתן האב לחם לבנו / וישב האיש בביתו
questions.selected_word: וילך האיש מביתו אל העיר / ויתן האב לחם לבנו / וישב האיש בביתו
questions.question: Which explanation best shows dependency across the pesukim?
choices: the receiver phrase explains where the earlier movement began
the movement phrase sets location or direction before the later action is understood
the later action changes the earlier subject into an object
the two pesukim are unrelated because they share no grammar
questions.correct_answer: the movement phrase sets location or direction before the later action is understood
questions.explanation: A multi-pasuk flow asks how one action prepares the next.

questions.question: What would change across the flow if מביתו were replaced by בביתו?
choices: only the spelling would change, but the flow would stay identical
all words would become nouns
the subject would become the receiver in every pasuk
the first pasuk would shift from leaving a place to being in a place
questions.correct_answer: the first pasuk would shift from leaving a place to being in a place
questions.explanation: A substitution can change how one pasuk connects to the next.
